t_ch: 嗯，{NICKNAME}？找我有什么事么？
t_en: Eh, {NICKNAME}? What brings you here?

t_ch: 你有兴趣来博物馆当志愿者吗？
t_en: Are you interested in volunteering at the museum?

t_ch: 博物馆？好像听布洛妮娅说过这个…不会就是她派你来的吧？
t_en: Museum? Bronya seems to have mentioned that before... Did she send you here?

t_ch: 我自己来的。
t_en: I sent myself here.

t_ch: 嗐…那我就跟你直说了吧。
t_en: Okay, I'll cut to the chase.

t_ch: 佩拉派我来的。
t_en: Pela sent me here.

t_ch: 佩拉…啊，我记得，是铁卫的那个情报官姑娘。
t_en: Pela... Oh, I remember her. She's the Intelligence Officer of the Silvermane Guard.

t_ch: 算了，谁派你来的都无所谓，我就和你直说了吧。
t_en: Forget it. It doesn't matter who has sent you here. I'll cut to the chase.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅派我来的。
t_en: Yeah, sure, Bronya sent me here. Totally.

t_ch: …哎。这家伙怎么总在这种奇怪的事情上固执得很。
t_en: *sigh* This woman is always so stubborn when it comes to odd things like this...

t_ch: 不过我就和你直说了吧——关于我不想当这什么志愿导览员的原因……
t_en: But let me just be straight with you — about why I don't wanna become a volunteer guide.

t_ch: 你想想啊——「贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆」里边的「历史」和「文化」俩词儿，哪个和我沾边？
t_en: I mean, just think about the name "Belobog History and Culture Museum." Do you really think I would fit anywhere near the "History" and the "Culture"?

t_ch: 所以嘛，倒也不是我不乐意帮忙，只是我实在不适合这种工作。不好意思啊，让你白跑一趟了。
t_en: It's not that I'm not willing to help, but I'm not suitable for the role. I'm sorry for making this a fruitless trip for you.

t_ch: 话不必说到这份上吧…
t_en: Come on, I'm sure you're more than cultured enough to be a volunteer guide.

t_ch: 这是事实嘛。我还是有自知之明的——小时候娜塔按着我脑袋让我读书，那简直跟要了我的命一样。
t_en: Well, the truth is, I'm simply not cut out for this line of work. Nat used to press my head down, forcing me to read when I was little. It was so suffocating.

t_ch: 你会指望这样的我给人介绍展品吗？我看还是算了吧，体验好不到哪儿去的。
t_en: Would you really want someone like me to introduce the exhibits to visitors? Let's just nip this idea in the bud. It won't end well for any of us.

t_ch: 没事，我也不懂贝洛伯格文化。
t_en: Don't worry. I, too, know nothing about Belobogian culture.

t_ch: 一码归一码。你虽然不是本地人，但看着还是有学养的，当个导览员对你来说肯定小菜一碟。
t_en: But you're different. Even though you're not a local, you still seem well-educated to me. So, being a guide must be easy-peasy for you.

t_ch: 至于我嘛——打我记事起，全下层区的人就都知道有个叫希儿的小无赖了。
t_en: As for me, everyone in the Underworld has seen me as a little rascal for as long as I can recall.

t_ch: 要是让他们在馆里见了我当导览员，那怕是要影响你们博物馆的声誉呢。
t_en: If they see me working there as a guide, I'm afraid the reputation of the museum will be tarnished.

t_ch: 可是布洛妮娅会伤心的啊。
t_en: But Bronya will be so sad about this.

t_ch: 为了她，你再考虑一下？
t_en: Can you reconsider... for Bronya's sake?

t_ch: 怎么，这就沉默了？
t_en: Um... Why the silence?

t_ch: …就你话多。
t_en: I'm thinking!

t_ch: 哎…行吧行吧，把那个申请表给我……
t_en: *sigh*... Fine, I'll do it. Just hand me the application form...

t_ch: …不，算了，你拿着吧。我说，你写。
t_en: Never mind, you keep the form for me and write down what I say verbatim.

t_ch: 乐意效劳！
t_en: I'm happy to help!

t_ch: 算你识相。
t_en: As you should.

t_ch: 你也太好懂了。
t_en: Huh, you are so easy to read.

t_ch: 啧！你、你别蹬鼻子上脸……
t_en: Tsk! Watch it! Don't push your luck...

t_ch: …啊啊，真是的！算了算了，我就是见不得那家伙哭——这下你满意了吧？
t_en: ...Aah! Fine! I just can't take it when she cries! Are you happy now?

t_ch: 你怎么不自己写？
t_en: Why don't you write it yourself?

t_ch: …我字不好看嘛。怎么，你有意见啊。
t_en: ...My handwriting is awful. Do you have a problem with that?

t_ch: 行了，开始吧——你可要好好把我的话记下来啊。
t_en: Okay, let's get down to business. Listen to me carefully.

t_ch: 第一问——您的姓名是？
t_en: The first question: What is your name?

t_ch: 希儿，没有姓。
t_en: Seele, no last name.

t_ch: 好的，希儿。
t_en: Then, Seele it is.

t_ch: 好了吗？好了就下一问吧。
t_en: Are you done? Let's move on to the next question when you're ready.

t_ch: 好的，希儿•没有姓。
t_en: Sure, Seele No-Last-Name.

t_ch: …你讨打！
t_en: Don't test me. You wanna mess around and find out?

t_ch: 下一问——您的个人经历？
t_en: The next question: What is your personal experience?

t_ch: 哎…我就知道。这种问题最难办了……
t_en: Ugh, I knew it. This is a tough one...

t_ch: …算了，你就填没有吧。我替地火办的那些个事，实在是不好说啊。
t_en: ...Forget it. Just say that I have no experience. I can't really disclose any of the jobs I have done for the Wildfire.

t_ch: 好的，那就写没有。
t_en: Fine. No experience it is.

t_ch: 行，下一问吧。
t_en: Okay. Next one, please.

t_ch: 好的，那就写不好说。
t_en: Okay, I'll write down "no comment" here.

t_ch: …你这家伙，故意的吧？
t_en: I can tell you're enjoying teasing me like this, don't you?

t_ch: 最后一问——您的技能特长？
t_en: The last question: What are your skills and expertise?

t_ch: 这问题，怎么一个比一个刁钻啊！我想想……
t_en: Why are the questions getting trickier? Let me think...

t_ch: …哎，你就填「解决纠纷」吧——这么写着不至于太吓人。
t_en: ...Hmm, just go ahead and put down "dispute resolution" — which won't sound too intimidating, I hope.

t_ch: 好的，「解决纠纷」。
t_en: Fine, "dispute resolution" it is.

t_ch: 好嘞，辛苦你了。
t_en: Thanks a lot.

t_ch: 好的，「太吓人，不予展示」。
t_en: Okay, I'll just put down "too scary to specify."

t_ch: …我现在反悔还来得及吗？
t_en: ...Is it too late to back out now?

t_ch: 都搞定了吧——让我看看？
t_en: I suppose it's done. Can I take a look?

t_ch: 人生总要留点惊喜…
t_en: Better to keep it a surprise.

t_ch: …那最好是惊喜，而不是什么惊吓。
t_en: ...I hope this surprise is gonna be a pleasant one.

t_ch: 行了，那我们一起去上层区吧，把这玩意交了去。
t_en: Well. Let's head to the Overworld and submit this thing.

t_ch: ——虽然我估计多半是通过不了审核的……
t_en: ...Though I reckon it will still be unlikely for me to pass the background check...

t_ch: 啧，这博物馆挺气派嘛…让我想起铆钉镇的那座孤儿院了。
t_en: Tsk, this museum looks so posh... It reminds me of that orphanage in Rivet Town.

t_ch: 艾丽斯…小姐，是吧？我是来交志愿导览员申请表的，给你就行了吗？
t_en: Are you... Miss Eris? I'm here to submit my volunteer guide application form. Should I give it to you?

t_ch: 是的，交给我就好！我这就把您的简历转交给佩拉小姐进行审核。
t_en: Yes. Please give it to me! I'll pass your resume on to Miss Pela right away!

t_ch: 还怪正经的…那我们就在这儿歇会儿吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: This seems serious... Let's take a break here, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 咦，希儿？你怎么在这里？
t_en: Eh, Seele? What are you doing here?
Um...

t_ch: 难道是{NICKNAME}带你过来的？
t_en: Did {NICKNAME} bring you here?

t_ch: 就是我。
t_en: Yep, that's me!

t_ch: 原来如此。之前我说什么都没用，看来还是要我们的大英雄{NICKNAME}出马呢。
t_en: I see. I've been trying to make you come for so long, but to no avail. Seems like {NICKNAME} is the only one who can convince you.

t_ch: 她自愿过来的。
t_en: She came of her own volition.

t_ch: 原来如此。没想到你最后还是做了这个决定…谢谢你，希儿。
t_en: I see. I'm surprised you have finally made up your mind... Thank you, Seele.

t_ch: 咳咳…现在该轮到我问问题了吧？
t_en: Ahem... I guess it's my turn to ask questions now?

t_ch: 我的申请多半是没通过审核吧？我是不是可以走了？
t_en: My application didn't pass the review, right? Can I just go now?

t_ch: 不。佩拉非常认可你的能力，所以就叫我来面试你了。
t_en: No. Pela was impressed by your ability, so she wanted me to have an interview with you.

t_ch: 你逃不掉咯，希儿！
t_en: No way for you to escape now, Seele!

t_ch: 可恶……
t_en: Darn it...

t_ch: 佩拉居然敢命令大守护者？
t_en: How come Pela has the gall to boss the Supreme Guardian around?

t_ch: 没有的事。大守护者本不该是那种高高在上的存在，再加上她是博物馆振兴计划的总负责人，我不觉得听她调遣有任何问题。
t_en: It's not like that. A Supreme Guardian should never be put on a pedestal. Since Pela is in charge of the museum revitalization plan, I have no problem following her instructions.

t_ch: 虽然她在和我谈这件事的时候确实很紧张就是了……
t_en: Still, she was quite nervous when she discussed this matter with me...

t_ch: 总之，希儿和{NICKNAME}，请你们和我一起到休息区吧——面试会在那里进行。
t_en: Anyway, let's head to the Rest Area, Seele and {NICKNAME}. The interview will be held there.

t_ch: 哎…时间还够，那就陪你走个过场吧。
t_en: Fine. I'll play along, but it's only because I'm not busy today.

t_ch: 谢谢你，希儿。至于辅助评定人{NICKNAME}——你先去忙自己手头的事吧。等你忙完了，我们就正式开始面试。
t_en: Thank you, Seele. As for {NICKNAME}, the assistant assessor, you may get on with your things first. We'll start the interview when you are ready.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你准备好了吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you ready?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 那么，请你做好准备，希儿。面试马上开始。
t_en: Please get ready, Seele. The interview will begin now.

t_ch: 不知怎么，竟然感觉有点紧张……
t_en: For some reason, I feel a bit nervous...

t_ch: 请再稍等我一会儿。
t_en: Please give me a moment.

t_ch: 没问题，我们会等你的。
t_en: No problem. We'll wait for you.

t_ch: 你们是在心灵感应吗？
t_en: Are you two communicating telepathically?

t_ch: 噗，这都哪儿跟哪儿啊……
t_en: Pfft, no way, telepathy ain't real!

t_ch: 倒是你——面试官布洛妮娅，你不会真的在试着和我心灵感应吧？
t_en: ...Wait a minute, you are not actually trying to read my mind, are you, Lady Bronya?

t_ch: …怎么连你也沉默了？
t_en: ...How come you aren't saying anything as well?

t_ch: 还有你——面试官布洛妮娅，你倒是说词儿啊！
t_en: And you, Lady Bronya. Why don't you say something?

t_ch: 懂了！一二三，木头人！
t_en: Oh I see, so we're playing a quiet game, huh?

t_ch: 噗，这都哪儿跟哪儿啊……
t_en: Pfft, no way, what are you even talking about!

t_ch: 倒是你——面试官布洛妮娅，你倒是说词儿啊！
t_en: Lady Bronya, why don't you say something?

t_ch: 我…我只是第一次以这种姿态和你对话，不知道该说什么好……
t_en: This... This is my first time speaking to you in this position, and I don't know what to say...

t_ch: 呃，那要不就到此为止？对你我都好——也甭浪费{NICKNAME}的时间了。
t_en: Um, should we end the interview here? It's good for both of us. Let's not take up any more of {NICKNAME}'s time.

t_ch: …不行！无论如何，流程还是要走的。
t_en: ...No way! We have to go through the entire process no matter what.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}——我想向你请教一下，面试官应该怎么提问比较好？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how do you think I should ask the questions as an interviewer?

t_ch: 根据简历内容提问就好。
t_en: Just ask questions based on the resume.

t_ch: 原来如此…那我就根据简历内容来提问吧——正好，也可以趁着这个机会再深入了解一下希儿。
t_en: I see... I'll ask the questions based on the resume then. This is also a great chance for me to get to know Seele better.

t_ch: 放松点，像和朋友一样交流。
t_en: You gotta loosen up, people! Just talk to each other like friends would.

t_ch: 原来如此…那我对着简历随意提问好了了——正好，也可以趁着这个机会再深入了解一下希儿。
t_en: I see... I'll ask some random questions based on the resume then. This is also a great chance for me to get to know Seele better.

t_ch: 难道我们之前还不够了解吗……
t_en: Don't we know each other well enough already...

t_ch: 首先是姓名…「希儿」，这确实是你的名字。
t_en: First of all, the name is "Seele," which is indeed your name.

t_ch: 首先是姓名…「希儿•没有姓」？贝洛伯格还有这种姓氏吗？
t_en: First of all, the name is "Seele No-Last-Name"? Is "No-Last-Name" an actual family name in Belobog?

t_ch: 咳咳…{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Ahem... {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 对不起，这是我的恶作剧。
t_en: Sorry about that. It's just a prank, sis.

t_ch: 看来你还不够了解贝洛伯格…
t_en: Seems like you don't know Belobog well enough...

t_ch: 咦？可我确实没有听过这么奇怪的——
t_en: Eh? I really haven't heard of such a weird surname...

t_ch: 啊，不是，我是想说「少见」！对不起，希儿，希望我没有冒犯到你……
t_en: Ah, I meant to say "uncommon surname"! Sorry, Seele. I hope you aren't offended...

t_ch: 什么啊，这明显是{NICKNAME}的恶作剧，你干嘛道歉！
t_en: This is clearly a prank pulled by {NICKNAME}! What are you even apologizing for?

t_ch: 噗…原来是这样。不过既然提到你的名字了，有一件事我一直很好奇——
t_en: Pfft... I see. Speaking of your name, there's something I've always been curious about...

t_ch: 其实，「希儿」这个名字在贝洛伯格本身也很少见…它有什么特殊的含义吗？
t_en: In fact, "Seele" itself is not a common name in Belobog... Does it have a special meaning?

t_ch: 这个名字嘛…是奥列格头儿给我起的。他说，这个名字在古代贝洛伯格语里的含义是「灵魂」。
t_en: Well... Oleg gave me the name. He said that it meant "soul" in ancient Belobogian.

t_ch: 我是不知道他到底想表达什么啦…不过，在下层那群闹事的地痞无赖眼里，我可能确实像一只来无影去无踪的鬼魂吧。
t_en: I'm not sure what he tried to convey through that name... But to all those thugs and gangsters in the Underworld, I'm like a specter always haunting them.

t_ch: 居然还有这样的意象…真是学到有趣的知识了。那么，我们来看下一问吧。
t_en: Wow, I'm surprised by its profound meaning. How fascinating! Now, the next question —

t_ch: 个人经历…居然是「没有」吗？但其实你经历过很多事情吧？
t_en: You have... no personal experience? You went through a lot, didn't you?

t_ch: 话是这么说没错。但绝大部分都是些不值一提的腌臜事…我想没有写进来的必要。
t_en: Indeed, but none of them is decent... I don't think it's necessary to include them.

t_ch: 嗯，我能理解——那我也就不刨根问底了。来看看接下来该问什么吧……
t_en: I understand, so I'm not going to probe. Let's see what's next...

t_ch: 个人经历…「不好说」？虽然我大概能理解，但为什么会这么写？
t_en: "No Comment" on your personal experience? I do get what it means, but why?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}——你有什么想说的吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, is there anything you want to say?

t_ch: 对不起，这也是我的恶作剧。
t_en: Sorry. It's another prank of mine.

t_ch: 希儿说，这样显得有神秘感…
t_en: Seele said it could make her look more mysterious...

t_ch: 别擅自把你的想法安在我头上啊？！
t_en: Don't pin the blame on me!

t_ch: 噗…好了好了，知道啦。我也就不刨根问底了，来看看接下来该问什么吧……
t_en: Pfft... Okay. I've got it. I'm not going to probe. Let's see what's next...

t_ch: 技能特长这里写着的是「解决纠纷」……
t_en: For skills and expertise, you wrote "dispute resolution"...

t_ch: 落到字面上就是这样。但我到底会做些什么…你在下层区想必也了解得够多了。
t_en: That's one way to describe it. I'm sure you know very well what I do in the Underworld.

t_ch: 我明白。不过，在博物馆的话，应该不会有那种程度的纠纷让你处理了。
t_en: I understand. However, there won't be such kind of dispute at the museum for you to deal with.

t_ch: 技能特长这里写着的是…「太吓人，不予展示」？这是……
t_en: For skills and expertise, you wrote "too scary to specify." And that means...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…对于大守护者的提问，你有什么高见吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}... Do you have a wise answer to the Supreme Guardian's question?

t_ch: 对不起，这还是我的恶作剧。
t_en: Sorry. It's yet another prank of mine.

t_ch: 这是希儿逼着我写的！
t_en: Seele forced me to write that!

t_ch: 喂喂喂——谁逼你了啊？！
t_en: Nobody forced you to do that!

t_ch: 哈哈，没想到你们这么要好呢。不过，希儿想要表达什么，我大概也能猜到了。
t_en: Haha, I'm surprised at how close you two are. In fact, I can more or less get what Seele is trying to say.

t_ch: 放心吧，在博物馆里的话…应该不会有你发挥「特长」的空间。
t_en: Don't worry. I don't think your "skills and expertise" will be put to use here.

t_ch: 有也没事，我能搞定。
t_en: Even if there were such a dispute, I could handle it.

t_ch: 不。要是真要遇到那种事，喊银鬃铁卫来就好了。
t_en: No. If there were such a dispute, you should need to summon the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 别忘了，你现在的职责之一是「守护」我这个大守护者——你可不能因为这种事情受伤。
t_en: Don't forget that protecting me is now one of your duties. You can't just gratuitously get yourself hurt.

t_ch: 哎，行吧…我知道了。
t_en: Okay okay... I got it.

t_ch: 好了，面试到此为止。接下来，我来公布面试结果——
t_en: Well, let's call it a day. I'll announce the result now.

t_ch: 肯定是没通过吧……
t_en: I'm sure I've failed...

t_ch: 通过了哦。欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆，希儿！
t_en: You've passed. Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum, Seele!

t_ch: 我现在…实在不知道该摆出什么表情。
t_en: I am... not sure how to respond to this.

t_ch: 恭喜你，希儿！
t_en: Congrats, Seele!

t_ch: 连你也这样啊。嗐，这事办得可真是……
t_en: Even you are doing this to me. Tsk, looks like I'm gonna be stuck here...

t_ch: 不错，这下事情有趣起来了。
t_en: The plot thickens!

t_ch: 啧…你就是想看我吃瘪吧。
t_en: Tsk... I'm sure you're just waiting to see me suffer.

t_ch: 你守护了布洛妮娅的笑容啊！
t_en: You've made Bronya happy!

t_ch: …哼，这回你倒是没说错。
t_en: ...Hmph, you're not wrong about that.

t_ch: 不管怎么样…只要她满意就够了。
t_en: Anyway... Everything's fine as long as she's happy.

t_ch: 嗯？你们在说什么悄悄话呢？
t_en: Eh? Are you two having a secret talk?

t_ch: …咳咳！没什么……
t_en: ...Ahem! Nothing...

t_ch: 喂，接下来我该做什么，布洛妮娅？快点安排任务吧。
t_en: Hey Bronya, what should I do next? Hurry up and assign some jobs to me.

t_ch: 等下先跟我来就好，我们要去办理一些手续。
t_en: Follow me later. We have to go through some formalities.

t_ch: 至于{NICKNAME}——谢谢你为本次面试提供的帮助。也多亏了你，我才能趁着这个机会更深入地了解希儿。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thank you for your help in the interview. Thanks to you, I've had the chance to get to know Seele better.

t_ch: 那么，我先带着她去办手续，先失陪了。以后也要常来博物馆里看看——当然，主要是来看看希儿的工作状态哦。
t_en: Please excuse me now, as I have to take Seele to complete the procedure. Remember to visit the museum often and check on Seele.

t_ch: 我至于那么让你不放心吗……
t_en: Do you really have so little trust in me...

t_ch: 嘿，你干嘛去！回来先跟娜塔莎确认下情况，别随便乱跑。
t_en: Hey, where are you going!? Don't think you can just run off — come back here and finish talking things through with Natasha.

t_ch: 喂！还有伤员需要帮忙呢，你别见死不救呀！
t_en: Hey! There are still wounded here that need our help!

t_ch: 情报都交换完啦？走，去那个什么「大矿区」看看！
t_en: Got all your intel? Come on, let's head over to that Great Mine, or whatever they call it.

t_ch: 我早就给安东尼娜矿头说过了，就不该让那些流浪者跟我们一起挖矿——要是没有这些惹事儿的家伙，我会在这里受委屈吗？
t_en: I told Antonia from the beginning: We never should have let those vagrants mine with us. I wouldn't be in this mess if it weren't for those thugs.

t_ch: 你少说两句吧！大家生活都不容易，现在正是互相帮助的时候。
t_en: Enough already. Nobody has it easy — this is a time to help each other out.

t_ch: 我记得你的舅舅还是大伯就是从铆钉镇逃出来的，他们也是流浪者对吧。你会把他们也赶出去吗？
t_en: I remember one of your uncles had to flee Rivet Town, which makes him a vagrant too, right? Would you drive him away?

t_ch: …那可是我的亲戚，其他的流浪者我才管不着。
t_en: ...He's family. The other vagrants aren't my problem.

t_ch: 你们看到我丈夫了吗？高高的、瘦瘦的、长得还很帅……
t_en: Have any of you seen my husband? He's a good-looking guy — tall and thin...

s_ch: 负责照顾伤员的「地火」成员
s_en: Wildfire Member Caring for the Injured
t_ch: 姐姐，这都什么时候了你还在开这种玩笑，别添乱了。
t_en: You again? Quit joking around! No need to add to the confusion.

t_ch: 哎呀，你还真的以为我在开玩笑啊？我看你在统计矿工名单，我才过来问你的。
t_en: *sigh* You think I'm joking? I saw you taking down the names of the miners — that's why I came over.

s_ch: 负责照顾伤员的「地火」成员
s_en: Wildfire Member Caring for the Injured
t_ch: …啊，你在我这里登记下名字吧。
t_en: Oh... You can put the name down here.

t_ch: 匹克——完蛋了，匹克那家伙还在里面睡觉。
t_en: Peak... he's done for. He was asleep in there.

t_ch: 你就别动弹了，娜塔莎医生让你好好休息，你这伤口又裂开了怎么办？再说了，伊蕾恩会把他带出来的，那姑娘靠谱。
t_en: Easy, Dr. Natasha told you to rest. What if your wound opens up again? Besides, Elaine will get him out, she's a reliable girl.

t_ch: 怎么伊蕾恩也没出来啊！走走走——
t_en: Elaine was in there too!? Quick, we have to—

t_ch: 哎呀，你就别瞎操心了，「地火」已经进去啦。
t_en: Calm down and stop worrying. Wildfire's already gone in.

t_ch: 这一天天的，都叫什么事儿啊……
t_en: What kind of days are we living through...

t_ch: 关于史瓦罗你还知道些什么？
t_en: What else do you know about Svarog?

t_ch: 史瓦罗？我哪会认识。再说了，现在地下这么乱，大家都各扫门前雪的，谁还管其他人的事儿啊！
t_en: Svarog? I don't know much. The underground is in turmoil, everyone's focused on their own issues. Who has time to get involved in someone else's affairs?

t_ch: 我听那些流浪者说，史瓦罗在炉心附近保护了流浪者的营地，我以为是什么大善人呢，嘁！就来干这些破事？
t_en: I heard the vagrants say Svarog was protecting their base near the Furnace Core. Tsk, and there I was thinking he was one of the good guys.

t_ch: 是你招惹了那些流浪者吗？
t_en: Did you bring those vagrants into the mine?

t_ch: 哈？你什么意思，你是在指责我吗？
t_en: Huh? What are you trying to say — that it's my fault?

t_ch: 你误会了。
t_en: You misunderstand.

t_ch: 别激动。
t_en: No need to get excited.

t_ch: 算了吧，我累了，没什么脾气了。
t_en: Forget it, I'm just tired, that's all.

t_ch: 这些流浪者就算是无家可归，但也是有尊严的，不可能一直接受别人的救济过活。
t_en: Even if those vagrants have no home to return to, they still have their dignity. They won't accept charity forever.

t_ch: 要想真正帮助他们越过难关，就应该给他们正当的工作。所以，我让这些流浪者加入了矿队。难道我做错了吗？
t_en: They needed honest work to get them through this hardship. So, I let them join the mining team. Can anyone really say I did wrong?

t_ch: 你没有错，只是事与愿违。
t_en: You did right, but maybe that's how the problem started.

t_ch: 背井离乡也是事与愿违，寄人篱下也是事与愿违…要怪就怪这地下日子过得不容易吧。
t_en: The problem started with them leaving home and being dependent on charity... If you want something to blame, blame life in the Underworld.

t_ch: 也许还可以处理得更好。
t_en: Maybe you could have handled it better.

t_ch: 哎，说到底他们选择拉帮结派，就是因为其他矿工还没有真正地接纳流浪者…我早该注意到的。
t_en: *sigh* When it boils down to it, they only chose to form a clique because the other miners never truly accepted them... I should've seen it.

t_ch: 你好像跟「地火」很熟？
t_en: You seem to be familiar with Wildfire.

t_ch: 他们是群靠谱的家伙。相信我，要是没有他们，这里早就跟铆钉镇一样被怪物占领了。
t_en: They're a reliable group. Trust me, if we didn't have them, this area would be packed with monsters already. Just like Rivet Town.

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 我准备带着矿队去找娜塔莎医生了，你们自己保重吧。
t_en: I'm about to take the mining team over to Dr. Natasha. Take care of yourselves.

t_ch: 我、我们、得、得救啦？
t_en: Are we s—saved?

t_ch: 得、得救啦！
t_en: You're s—saved!

t_ch: 不、不要，不要学我！
t_en: Hey, n—no need for that!

t_ch: 先去找个安全的地方吧。
t_en: Go and find somewhere safe.

t_ch: 好、好的，谢谢你！
t_en: O—Okay, thanks!

t_ch: 是你啊，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}。这里不适合聊天，有事长话短说吧。
t_en: Ah, young {F#lady}{M#man}. This isn't the best place for a chat. Let's keep things brief.

t_ch: 那个史瓦罗是何方神圣？
t_en: What's so great about Svarog?

t_ch: 他啊，他是旧时代的智能机器人，地下刚开始建设时就存在了——我知道的就这么些。
t_en: Svarog? He's an artificially intelligent being from the Old World. He was here when the underground was first constructed. That's about all I know.

t_ch: 现在的史瓦罗，算是据守炉心枢纽的一桩硬茬。很多流浪者在枢纽附近建立了营地，寻求史瓦罗的保护。
t_en: Now he guards the Furnace Core hub. Many vagrants have established bases in the vicinity, seeking Svarog's protection.

t_ch: 矿区里发生了什么？
t_en: What happened in the mine?

t_ch: 事情的大概你已经听娜塔莎讲过了吧？矿工和流浪者因为矿脉大打出手，史瓦罗的手下将所有的人都赶了出去。
t_en: You got the gist from Natasha, right? The miners and vagrants came to blows over the vein, then Svarog's subordinates pushed everyone back.

t_ch: 只是赶出去吗？
t_en: Just pushed them back?

t_ch: 嗯，虽然手段粗暴了一些，但是它们只是将矿民和流浪者赶了出去。
t_en: Well, perhaps their methods were lacking in finesse, but that's essentially what they did.

t_ch: 史瓦罗不是来抢矿脉的吗？
t_en: So Svarog wasn't after the vein?

t_ch: 就像你看到的，史瓦罗把所有人赶出去之后就离开了。很显然，他对这个矿脉并不感兴趣。
t_en: You saw for yourself — after he pushed everyone back, he disappeared. It was clear: He didn't have any interest in the vein itself.

t_ch: 要我说，他也算是制止了地下的冲突，只不过方式简单粗暴，毫无人味。
t_en: If you want my take, he prevented another Underworld conflict, even if the execution was rough around the edges.

t_ch: 他的行为就像是在说「只要把人都赶走了，就没有冲突了」。
t_en: His behavior says something about his thinking: "As long as everyone's been driven out, the conflict is over."

t_ch: 你想找布洛妮娅说什么？
t_en: What do you want to say to Bronya?

t_ch: 哈，我听说她是追捕你们的银鬃铁卫，还以为你们之间挺不对付的…你在担心铁卫丫头？
t_en: Well, I heard that she's a Silvermane Guard that was sent to arrest you. I expected you to be less friendly around each other... Are you worried about her?

t_ch: 我们现在是合作关系。
t_en: We're working together now.

t_ch: 哦，现在是合作关系。我明白了，放心吧，我不为难她。
t_en: Oh, working together? Understood. Rest easy, I won't make things difficult for her.

t_ch: 威胁小姑娘是不好的。
t_en: Don't threaten her.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，你的担心纯属多余，那铁卫丫头是不会被我这个退伍老兵给威胁到的。
t_en: Hahahaha, you needn't worry! That Silvermane girl wouldn't be afraid of an old veteran anyway.

t_ch: 我很担心她。
t_en: Very much so.

t_ch: 是吗？这是我没想到的。不过你放心吧，我不为难她。
t_en: Really? I'm surprised. Still, you can rest easy, I won't make things difficult for her.

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫已经消失很久了，作为地下住民，我只是有一些问题想要问问她而已。
t_en: We haven't seen a Silvermane Guard down here for a long time now. I just wanna ask her a few questions, nothing more.

t_ch: 我没有什么要问的了。
t_en: I've nothing to ask.

t_ch: 时候不早了，你们也早点回镇上休息吧。
t_en: It's getting late. You should go back to the town and get some rest.

t_ch: 地火、流浪者、史瓦罗…下层区的变化远比我想象中的更加激烈。唉，银鬃铁卫离开之后，地下到底经历了什么？
t_en: Wildfire, the vagrants, Svarog... The changes in here are greater than I ever imagined. *sigh* What exactly did the Underworld go through after the Silvermane Guards left?

t_ch: …抱歉，你有话要跟我说？
t_en: ...Sorry, did you have something you wanted to say to me?

t_ch: 你觉得奥列格想聊什么？
t_en: What do you think Oleg wants to talk about?

t_ch: 他想要聊的内容，我心里大概有数。就算奥列格不找我，我也会主动找到他的。
t_en: I think I know what it will concern. Even if Oleg wasn't looking for me, I would've looked for him.

t_ch: 作为地下住民，他们肯定有很多抱怨想要发泄；而作为银鬃铁卫，我也有很多情况需要确认，仅此而已。
t_en: I'm certain that the residents of the underground will have a lot they need to vent about. And as a Silvermane Guard, I have a lot that I need to ascertain. Nothing more.

t_ch: 你怎么看这个「史瓦罗大佬」？
t_en: What do you think of this Boss Svarog?

t_ch: 按照奥列格的说法，那是参加过七百年前保卫战争的机器人，拥有与人类接近的智能，统治着地下机械…
t_en: According to Oleg, he's a robot that took part in the War of Defense over seven hundred years ago. He possesses a near-human intelligence, and rules over all the machinery in the underground...

t_ch: 筑城者们曾在「{RUBY_B#与反物质军团的战争}那场战争{RUBY_E#}」中创造了惊人的机械士兵，但大部分机械都在惨烈的战争中损坏，技术也没有保留下来。
t_en: During {RUBY_B#War Against the Antimatter Legion}the war{RUBY_E#}, the Architects created astonishing mechanical soldiers, but the fight was so terrible that most were damaged or destroyed. The technology was lost.

t_ch: …如果奥列格所言非虚，那么史瓦罗就是从旧时代里遗留下来的筑城者产物。
t_en: ...If what Oleg says is true, then Svarog is an Architect creation leftover from the Old Era.

t_ch: 你和希儿配合得很好。
t_en: You and Seele work well together.

t_ch: 有吗？我倒没什么印象了。我接受过严格的军事训练，能在任何情况下与战士们协同作战…
t_en: We do? I hadn't realized. I've received rigorous military training — coordinating with soldiers under any circumstances is easy for me...

t_ch: 你再坦率一点吧。
t_en: You can say what you mean.

t_ch: 真的只是训练的原因？
t_en: So it's really only the training?

t_ch: 我承认那个希儿天赋过人，我们两个…配合得确实不错，不过就仅此而已了。
t_en: I admit, Seele is talented. We... do work well together. But that's all there is to it.

t_ch: 我要问就这些了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 谢谢你的关心，你们先回镇上吧，我和奥列格谈完就回去。
t_en: Hey... Thanks for your concern. You should head back to the town, and... I'll do the same when Oleg and I are finished.

t_ch: 是你们啊…看样子，冲突已经暂时解决了。太好了，矿区里的伤员再增加下去，我也不知道该怎么办了。要是那个人在的话……
t_en: Oh, it's you... It looks like the conflict is over for now. Thank goodness, if the numbers of wounded had kept climbing, I don't know what we'd have done. If only he'd have been here...

t_ch: …不是想这些事情的时候，我现在应该专注在救治伤员的事上。
t_en: ...Now's not the time for those thoughts. I need to focus on treating the injured.

t_ch: 有什么可以帮你的吗？
t_en: Can I help you with anything?

t_ch: 你们已经做得足够多了。救治伤员的事情就交给医生来做吧。
t_en: You've done enough. Treating the injured is doctor's work.

t_ch: 要是没有受伤，就回去好好休息吧。放心，在需要你们帮助的时候，我也不会客气。
t_en: So if you're not hurt yourself, you should go back and get some rest. Relax, if I need your assistance, I won't hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 那个人是？
t_en: And who is "he?"

t_ch: …能当作姐姐没有提到吗？现在不是谈论这个的时候。
t_en: ...Could you pretend like you didn't hear anything? Now's not the time.

t_ch: 辛苦医生了。
t_en: Thank you, doctor.

t_ch: 也辛苦你们了。才刚到地下就解决了这么大的冲突，真不知道要怎么感谢你们才好。
t_en: The same to you and your friends. You've just arrived and already helped resolve such a sizable conflict. I don't know how to thank you.

t_ch: 先回镇上休息吧。
t_en: You'd best get some rest back in town.

t_ch: 你们挺有本事的呀。有需要我帮忙的地方，尽管开口。
t_en: You guys have some moves. If you need my help with anything, just shout.

t_ch: 现在终于相信我们了吗？
t_en: So you finally believe us?

t_ch: 哼，相信哪有那么容易。我只是觉得你们人还行。至少你们拿得出实际行动，不仅仅是嘴上说说。
t_en: Hmph, it'll take more than that. But you're not as bad as I took you for. You walk the talk.

t_ch: 现在就需要你的帮忙。
t_en: We need your help.

t_ch: 你还真不客气啊。
t_en: Huh, couldn't even give it a minute, could you?

t_ch: 我忙一天了，实在没精力再折腾了。今天先让我喘口气吧，明天我来找你。
t_en: I've been on my feet all day. I don't have the energy for more headaches right now. Let me catch my breath and I'll come find you tomorrow.

t_ch: 对了，我再确认一下，那个叫布洛妮娅的大小姐…真的是银鬃铁卫吗？
t_en: Oh, I wanted to ask you — that Bronya girl... Is she really a Silvermane Guard?

t_ch: 是，而且她是领导。
t_en: Yes, she's their leader.

t_ch: 啊？她能带兵？那还挺了不起的嘛。
t_en: Oh? She heads up the force? Hm... Not bad.

t_ch: 你不相信她的本事？
t_en: You don't think she's got the talent?

t_ch: 没有，她确实身手了得，看得出经受了严格的训练。
t_en: No, no, she's got skill alright, I can tell she's got training.

t_ch: 只是…我以为银鬃铁卫都是冷酷无情的，他们让整个下层自生自灭。和她给我的感觉…不大一样。
t_en: It's just... I thought that Silvermane Guards were all cold and unfeeling — they left the Underworld to perish. But she... doesn't give me that impression.

t_ch: 就这样吧。我还有事要向首领报告，不陪你们了。
t_en: Anyway, I've got stuff to report to the chief. See you around.

t_ch: 你们别过来啊！此路不通，谁都别想进来！
t_en: Don't come any closer! The road's blocked — stay where you are!

t_ch: 你们藏在里面干嘛，不知道矿区现在出什么事儿了吗？
t_en: ...Whoa, easy! Why are you hiding in there? Don't you know what happened in the mine?

t_ch: 跟我们有啥关系？别把我们跟那群地痞流氓混为一谈！
t_en: What's that got to do with us? Do you think we're the same as those thugs?

t_ch: 里面有人受伤了吗？这里有医生，你们把伤员放出来吧！
t_en: Is anyone with you injured? We have a doctor. She can care for your wounded!

t_ch: 说得好听，你敢保证没人是来找茬的吗？休想，谁都别想进来！
t_en: Yeah right, and you can guarantee that no one there with you is out for blood? I don't believe it. Stay where you are!

t_ch: 嗨，这群家伙……
t_en: *sigh* These guys...

t_ch: 哼，眼看形势不对，撒腿就跑…也就这点出息了。
t_en: Hmph, fleeing at the first sign of danger... that's all they're good for.

t_ch: 他们是在守着这部升降梯吗？咱们能用它直接下去吗？
t_en: Were they guarding this elevator? Can we use it to go down?

t_ch: 听他们刚才谈话的内容，似乎是出了什么故障。去亲自检查一下也无妨。
t_en: From what they were saying, it sounds like there's a fault. There's no harm in taking a look though.

t_ch: 嗯…确实是故障了，一点反应都没有。
t_en: Mm... so there is a fault. It's not responding at all.

t_ch: 嘁，算了。只能绕远路了。
t_en: Tsk, forget it. We'll have to go the long way around.

t_ch: 欸？这是什么东西，是刚才那几个家伙丢下的吗……
t_en: Huh? What's this... Did those guys leave it behind...?

t_ch: 呜…呜哇！
t_en: Wh—Whoa!

t_ch: 这、这是什么？！
t_en: What the heck!?

t_ch: 哔嘟…正在搜索可能的使用者……
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Searching for potential user...

t_ch: 哔嘟…检测到声纹，正在检索声纹对应身份信息——天啊，居然还是个外星人。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Voiceprint detected. Looking up corresponding identity information... Oh my, it's an alien.

t_ch: 什、什么情况，这家伙说话了？而且它还知道我们不是本地人？
t_en: Hey, it talks! And it knows that we aren't from this planet?

t_ch: 肯定不是这个世界的科技。看——它外壳上还有星际和平公司的标志。
t_en: It doesn't seem to be technology from here. Look — it even has an Interastral Peace Corporation logo on it.

t_ch: 说不定是被从前的星际旅行者遗留在这里的老古董。
t_en: Perhaps it's an antiquity left behind by some past interstellar traveler.

t_ch: 哔嘟…你才是老古董！你全家都是老古董！
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Your mom's an antiquity!

t_ch: 哔嘟…无所谓啦，无所谓啦。外星人，你们知道星际和平公司？那太好了。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Whatever, whatever. So you aliens know about the Interastral Peace Corporation? That's great.

t_ch: 哔嘟…自我介绍。我是「家用寻物仪『理查德』•版本<unbreak>0.63</unbreak>•专家型•年度豪华版」，昵称「查宝」。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Self introduction: I am Richard, the Home-Use Object Finder. Model information: Version <unbreak>0.63</unbreak>, Professional model, Annual Deluxe package. Nickname: "Findie."

t_ch: 「家用寻物仪」？原来「公司」还有这种产品啊……
t_en: A Home-Use Object Finder? Who knew the IPC made these kinds of products...

t_ch: 哔嘟…本机脑波扫描模组已损坏。请在保修期承诺的二百三十二年零八十四天内将本产品送至「星际和平家电」门店进行免费保修维护。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... My brainwave scanner module is damaged. Please send me to an Interastral Peace Appliances retail store near you for free repair within the warranty period of <unbreak>232</unbreak> years <unbreak>84</unbreak> days.

t_ch: 外星人，请带我去「星际和平家电」门店进行维修——
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Alien, please take me to an Interastral Peace Appliances retail store to conduct repairs—

t_ch: 什么啊，原来这家伙已经坏掉了…怎么办，要带它一起走吗？
t_en: What!? This fella's already broken... What should we do, take it with us?

t_ch: 带上吧，指不定能帮上什么忙。
t_en: Let's take it. Maybe it could be of some use.

t_ch: 哔嘟…对对对，太对了。需要找东西的时候，呼唤查宝就对了。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Yes, yes, yes. When you need to find something you can't see, just call upon Findie. That's me.

t_ch: 负重已经过高了……
t_en: My inventory is already maxed out...

t_ch: 虽然但是，我们应该没有这种机制吧……
t_en: Ugh, you need to log out once in a while.

t_ch: 把它带上吧，反正也不占地方。就算它真的坏了，这里四处都是垃圾箱。
t_en: Let's take it with us. It doesn't take up much room. And if it really is broken, there are trashcans everywhere.

t_ch: 哔嘟…不要垃圾箱！对对对，需要找东西的时候，呼唤查宝就对了。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Not a trashcan! Yes, yes, yes. When you need to find something you can't see, just call upon Findie. That's me.

t_ch: 被堵得严严实实的…怎么办？要把这辆矿车炸掉吗？
t_en: It's completely blocked by the mine cart... What do we do? Can we blow it up?

t_ch: 慢着！这些都是矿队的资产，可不兴随便破坏。还是想办法把它修好吧。
t_en: Easy! It belongs to the mining team. We can't just destroy it. We should try and fix it.

t_ch: 话是这么说…你知道该怎么修吗？
t_en: Easier said than done... Do you know how?

t_ch: 那当然，下层区哪个人不知道矿车的构造？让我检查一下……
t_en: Yeah, of course, every Underworlder worth their salt knows how to fix a mine cart. Let me see...

t_ch: …嗯，连结车体跟底盘的「销子」坏了，后轮的「轮对轴承」也断了。一看就是史瓦罗的喽啰干的。
t_en: ...Hmm. Even the pin connecting the body to the chassis is broken, not to mention the rear wheelset bearing. This looks like the work of Svarog's minions.

t_ch: 走吧，来的路上我看到不少废弃的矿车，回去找找有没有能用的部件。
t_en: Come on, we passed a lot of discarded mine carts on the way over, let's see if there are any components we can recycle.

t_ch: 得在周围找找能用的替换部件。嘁，真是浪费时间……
t_en: We need to find spare parts we can use nearby. Argh, we're wasting time...

t_ch: 哔嘟…好伙伴们，需要找东西吗？查宝可以帮忙。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Friends, do you need to find something? Findie can help.

t_ch: …这家伙怎么自顾自地跳出来了？你的那什么功能不是已经损坏了吗？
t_en: ...How come you just jumped out on your own, little fella? Isn't your module thingy broken?

t_ch: 哔嘟…脑波扫描模组确实已经损坏。但人工输入搜寻功能仍可正常运行。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... My brainwave scanner module is indeed broken. However, manual input for search is still operable.

t_ch: 来吧来吧，输入你们想要寻找的物件名字。查宝来帮忙。
t_en: Come on. Input the name of the object you are looking for. Findie can help.

t_ch: 嚯，这么自信？那咱就试试吧。
t_en: Oh, well aren't you confident? Let me give it a try then.

t_ch: 输入界面在哪？哦，找到了……
t_en: Where's the input interface? Ah, found it...

t_ch: 「矿车销子」…「矿车轮对」…确认…这样就行了？
t_en: Mine Cart Pin... Mine Cart Wheelset... Confirm... Just like that?

t_ch: 哔嘟…正在处理请求……
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Request is being processed...

t_ch: 哔嘟…匹配成功…开始搜索遗失物件「矿车销子」……
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Match successful... Initiating search for lost item: Mine Cart Pin...

t_ch: 哔嘟！哔嘟！发现近似物件，请遵循「家用寻物仪安全使用指南」开始搜索指引。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep! Item found. Please commence search according to the Home-Use Object Finder Safety Manual.

t_ch: 让我看看…嗯，这部件勉强能用，这小东西还挺能干的嘛。
t_en: Let me see... Yeah, we can make do with this part. This little machine isn't half bad.

t_ch: 哔嘟…怎么样，对查宝刮目相看了吧。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... What's that? Is that a hint of respect for Findie?

t_ch: 这也太方便了吧？以后如果丹恒再走丢了的话，是不是也能用查宝找到他？
t_en: How convenient! So next time Dan Heng gets lost, we can just use Findie to find him, right?

t_ch: 哔嘟…目前暂不支持搜寻有机生命体的功能。详情请致电「星际和平家电」门店咨询。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Biological life form search is currently not supported. If you have any questions, please call Interastral Peace Appliances.

t_ch: …想法倒是不错。继续找下个部件吧。
t_en: ...Well, it was a good idea. Let's look for the next part.

t_ch: 哔嘟…开始搜索遗失物件「矿车轮对」……
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Initiating search for lost item: Mine Cart Wheelset...

t_ch: 哔嘟！哔嘟！发现近似物件，请遵循「家用寻物仪安全使用指南」开始搜索指引。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep! Item found. Please commence search according to the Home-Use Object Finder Safety Manual.

t_ch: 这下就齐全了！
t_en: That's everything!

t_ch: 帮大忙了，查宝！
t_en: You were a big help, Findie!

t_ch: 哔嘟…哔嘟…不用谢。「星际和平家电」，你最可靠的伙伴。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... It was nothing. Interastral Peace Appliances, your most trustworthy companion.

t_ch: 哔嘟…本次搜索完毕，进入休眠模式。请及时为「家用寻物仪」充电。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Current search complete. *yawn* Entering sleep mode. Please recharge the Home-Use Object Finder.

t_ch: …咱们运气可真好，要是没有查宝，估计得花上半天才能找到这些零件吧？
t_en: ...We're pretty lucky, huh? Without Findie, it probably would've taken half the day to find these parts.

t_ch: 那倒也不至于…不过这小玩意确实挺方便的。好了，快回去修矿车吧。
t_en: Well, not that long... But that little machine definitely made it a lot easier. Alright, let's go back and fix the mine cart.

t_ch: 这辆怎么样？
t_en: How about this one?

t_ch: 我看看……
t_en: Let me have a look...

t_ch: 不行，这辆车的部件都锈蚀得很严重了。再在旁边找找吧。
t_en: No good, all the components here are rusted. Let's keep looking.

t_ch: 这辆行吗？
t_en: What about this one?
Hmm...

t_ch: 轮子已经坏彻底了，不过连结部件勉强还能用，先卸下来吧。
t_en: The wheels are completely broken, but the connecting component should be pretty much okay. Let's remove it.

t_ch: 这辆好像还挺新的？
t_en: This one looks pretty new.

t_ch: 我检查一下……
t_en: Let me see...

t_ch: 销子不见了？流浪者连这玩意都要偷吗……
t_en: Ugh, where's the pin? Is there anything those vagrants won't steal?

t_ch: 还好底盘跟轮对保存得还算完整。帮我一把，把这俩轮子卸下来。
t_en: At least the chassis and the wheelset are relatively intact. Here, give me a hand removing the wheels.

t_ch: 众人一起修好了矿车……
t_en: You all fix the mine cart together...

t_ch: 这下应该就大功告成啦！
t_en: Alright, mission accomplished!

t_ch: 哼，手脚还挺麻利的嘛，我还以为你们个个都是娇生惯养，没干过粗活呢。
t_en: Hmph, quick work. Y'know, I didn't think you guys had worked a day in your lives, but you know how to get your hands dirty.

t_ch: 准备发车咯！
t_en: The cart is about to depart!

t_ch: 前面的矿道估计已经被机器人占领了，走路小心点儿。
t_en: The robots will have seized the road ahead by now. We should go carefully.

t_ch: 这条路对吗？前面好像有路障。
t_en: Is this the right way? It looks like there's a roadblock up ahead.

t_ch: 别说的好像你来过一样。走，离近点看看。
t_en: ...I'll be the judge of that. Come on, let's take a closer look.

t_ch: 这下就齐全了！
t_en: That's everything!

t_ch: 很好，回去修矿车吧。
t_en: Great, let's head back and fix the cart.

t_ch: 希儿要亲自动手吗？
t_en: Are you gonna do it yourself?

t_ch: 不然呢？下层区干什么都得自食其力，你们也学着点。
t_en: Why wouldn't I? You've gotta be self-reliant down here. Watch, you might learn something.

t_ch: 和人类为敌的机器人？我从没听说过这种事，机器应该是无条件服从人类指令的……
t_en: Robots aligned against humans? I've never heard of such a thing. Machines should obey humans unconditionally...

t_ch: 在地下，你没见过的事可太多了，「大小姐」。
t_en: That's not the only thing you won't have heard of down here, princess.

t_ch: 哎哟哟哟，你们又是什么人啊？知道这是谁的地盘吗！
t_en: Aha, and who do we have here? Do you know whose territory this is!?

t_ch: 少废话，这儿肯定不是你们的地盘，你们一起上吧，我赶时间！
t_en: Shut it, this isn't your territory. I'm in a hurry — I'll take you all in one go!

t_ch: 矿车部件
t_en: Mine Cart Part

t_ch: 刚才的标签，对应的应该就是这个箱子！等一下——虎克好像闻到了什么味道！
t_en: The label must've been on this box! Wait a minute... What's that smell?

t_ch: 是调味料的气味！你闻闻，这个味道，好像盐烧洞螈……
t_en: *sniff* It smells like condiments! Mmm, this smells like salt-grilled olm...

t_ch: 这和犯人有关吗 ？
t_en: What does this have to do with the culprit?

t_ch: 一般人身上会有这么明显的气味吗？
t_en: Not too many people smell like this...

t_ch: 我饿了。
t_en: I'm hungry.

t_ch: 找到犯人之前，不许饿！
t_en: There won't be any eating until we find the culprit!

t_ch: 怎么办，小偷好像把老爹的宝贝带走了…这根断掉的探针，好像就是探测器上的！
t_en: I think the thief took daddy's treasure... and I think that this damaged probe is from the detector!

t_ch: 老爹的宝贝…不会已经被卖掉了吧？
t_en: Has daddy's treasure been sold already?

t_ch: 小偷到底会去哪呢？唔…虎克虎克，再努力想想……
t_en: Where could the thief have gone? Hmm... think, Hook, think...

t_ch: 大概是急着去销赃吧。
t_en: Probably trying to sell off the spoils.

t_ch: 应该还没有跑出大矿区。
t_en: They probably haven't left the Great Mine yet.

t_ch: 啊，对了！在来矿区之前，虎克还在路上看到了桑博叔叔…这件事会不会跟他有关系啊？
t_en: Oh yeah! I saw Mr. Sampo on the way to the mine when I came here... Could he be involved in this?

t_ch: 绝对和他有关系！
t_en: It definitely has something to do with him!

t_ch: 你早说这个名字我都不用查了。
t_en: Mentioning that name sooner would've saved us a lot of time.

t_ch: 虎克来带路，桑博叔叔应该就在那个方向！
t_en: Follow Hook, Mr. Sampo should be that way!

t_ch: 也许已经畏罪自尽…
t_en: Perhaps they are off punishing themselves for their crime...

t_ch: 我相信桑博是清白的！
t_en: I'm sure Sampo is innocent!

t_ch: …实在没有底气这么说啊。
t_en: ...Heh, who could ever say that confidently.

t_ch: 你！就是你，别跑！你被鼹鼠党征用了！
t_en: You! Hey, you, stop! You are now a conscript of The Moles!

t_ch: 虎克的小跟班…
t_en: Hook's little sidekick...

t_ch: 什么跟班，是二把手…等等，现在不是说这个的时候！
t_en: I'm not a sidekick! I'm second-in-command! Argh. You know what? No. This ain't the time.

t_ch: 会变装魔法的小朋友…
t_en: The young little master of disguise...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我「千面尤利安」的名号…等等，现在不是说这个的时候！
t_en: Heehee, I, Julian of a Thousand Faces... Wait, it's not the time for this!

t_ch: 大事不好了！虎克大人她，一个人带着洞洞机跑到大矿区里去了！
t_en: We got trouble! The boss went to the Great Mine alone with her Diggertron!

t_ch: 而且还不让其他人一起跟过去，好像是有什么危险的事…你得替我们去看看！
t_en: And she refused to let anyone go with her. I feel like she's doing something dangerous... You have to go check on her for us!

t_ch: 她去矿区干嘛？
t_en: Why would she go to the mines?

t_ch: 费斯曼老爹就在矿区里，虎克大人好像是听说老爹出事了才冲进去的。
t_en: Fersman is in the mines. It seems like the boss heard some bad news about him, then just ran in there.

t_ch: 连洞洞机都带上了，不会是打算和流浪者干架吧……
t_en: She even took her Diggertron. Is she preparing for a fight with the Vagrants...

t_ch: 矿区里有很多坏人，娜塔莎姐姐说了，小孩子不能随便进去！你快替我们去看看！
t_en: There's lots of bad guys in the mines. Miss Natasha said kids ain't allowed to go in! Go check on her for us now!

t_ch: 费斯曼老爹是谁？
t_en: Who is Fersman?

t_ch: 就是虎克的老爹，也是鼹鼠党大家的老爹！费斯曼老爹对每个人都很好，总之就是个大好人！
t_en: He's Hook's daddy, and he's like a daddy to everyone in The Moles! He's like... a great person!

t_ch: 洞洞机？
t_en: Diggertron?

t_ch: 洞洞机就是洞洞机啊！只要虎克大人带着它，总免不了一场恶战……
t_en: Diggertron is Diggertron! As long as the boss has it with her, you can expect a good fight...

t_ch: 费斯曼老爹就在矿区里，虎克大人好像是听说老爹出事了才冲进去的。
t_en: Fersman is in the mines. It seems like the boss heard some bad news about him, then just ran in there.

t_ch: 矿区里有很多坏人，小孩子不能随便进去！你快替我们去看看！
t_en: There's lots of bad guys in the mines. Miss Natasha said kids ain't allowed to go in! Go check on her for us now!

t_ch: 费斯曼老爹是谁？
t_en: Who is Fersman?

t_ch: 就是虎克的老爹，也是鼹鼠党大家的老爹！费斯曼老爹对每个人都很好，总之就是个大好人！
t_en: He's Hook's daddy, and he's like a daddy to everyone in The Moles! He's like... a great person!

t_ch: 好吧，不要担心，我马上过去。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll head there right away.

t_ch: 你让我去我就去，岂不是很没…
t_en: Why would I take orders from a little...

t_ch: 拜托你了，快点吧！
t_en: Thank you! Please hurry!

t_ch: …你哪位？
t_en: ...Who are you?

t_ch: 鼹鼠党的二把手「千面尤——等等，现在不是说这个的时候！
t_en: You kidding? It's me! The second-in-command of The Moles? Julian of a Thousand— You know what? No. It ain't the time for this.

t_ch: 漆黑虎克大人的事就是我的事。
t_en: Alright, I'll go help Pitch-Dark Hook the Great.

t_ch: 哎呀，人命关天！还计较什么呀！
t_en: Someone's life is at risk! Forget the minor details!

t_ch: 你不是找我有要紧事吗？
t_en: Don't you have urgent matters for me?

t_ch: 冷静，冷静！大家都是为了讨个生计，咱好好坐下来聊聊不好吗？
t_en: Calm down, calm down! We're all here to work for a living. Why not sit down and talk it out?

t_ch: 说得可真好听！我们把你当领队，你就这么对待我们？
t_en: Save it! We treated you as our leader, but look how you treated us?

t_ch: 吃里扒外的东西！
t_en: Such a rat!

t_ch: 你把矿脉的消息卖给了那个疯婆子安东尼娜！明明是我们先…现在好了，我们还得反过来给那群家伙打工！
t_en: You sold the intel about the vein to Antonia, but we discovered it first! Now, we lost the mine and we have to work for those guys!

t_ch: 叛徒！
t_en: Traitor!

t_ch: 咋了，没话说了？你承认自己把秘密出卖给矿队了？
t_en: Well, nothing to say for yourself, huh? So you admit that you sold the intel to the mining team?

t_ch: 你们这些坏家伙！不准欺负老爹！
t_en: Go away, you brutes! Don't bully my daddy!

t_ch: 虎克小鬼？不不不，你搞反了，是你老爹有错在先。不信你自己问他！
t_en: Little Hook? No, no, you have it wrong. It's your daddy who bullied us first. Ask him if you don't believe me!

t_ch: 老爹…？
t_en: Daddy...?

t_ch: 没错，是我告诉安东尼娜的…但这矿脉是我第一个发现的，我想怎么处置是我自己的自由！
t_en: Yes, I told Antonia about the vein... but I was the first one to discover it. I have the right to do anything with it.

t_ch: 嚯，了不起啊！可你不就是仗着手里那个「探测仪」吗？等我们把那玩意抢过来，看你还敢不敢嚣张！
t_en: Tsk, it's only because you have that detector. Let's see who's boss when we take it from you!

t_ch: 离、离我远点！我这都是为了大局着想，哪像你们成天就想着四处滋事？
t_en: Back off! I was thinking about the bigger picture when I did that, unlike you all who just look for trouble every day!

t_ch: 依我看，你这老头就是欠教训。兄弟们，抄家伙！
t_en: Well, I think someone needs to teach you a lesson. Guys, get him!

t_ch: 有虎克在，你们休想！
t_en: You'll never lay a finger on daddy without passing through Hook!

t_ch: 鼹鼠大军在此，不得放肆！
t_en: The Moles are here. Surrender now!

t_ch: 我来了！全都住手！
t_en: I'm here! Everybody, stop!

t_ch: 啊，是你！快，快来帮虎克的忙！
t_en: Ah, it's you! Come and help Hook!

t_ch: 嘁，又来一个倒霉蛋？多你一个不多，都来尝尝本大爷的厉害！
t_en: Tsk, another punching bag? Fine by me! I'll kick all your butts!

t_ch: 大家消消气，给我个面子…
t_en: Take it easy, everyone...

t_ch: 哎哟喂，别打了别打了！这小鬼怎么这么厉害，哎哟，疼！
t_en: Ow! Stop it, stop it! How's a kid so strong... Ah! Owwww!

t_ch: 滚出避难所！虎克不想再看到你们啦！
t_en: Get out of the shelter! I never want to see you again!

t_ch: 嘁…你们给我等着！撤，我们先撤！
t_en: Tsk... Just you wait! Let's go for now!

t_ch: 老爹，你受伤了没？
t_en: Daddy, are you hurt?

t_ch: 咳、咳…一点擦伤而已。虎克呢？你还好吧？
t_en: *coughing* ...Just a few scratches. How about you, Hook?

t_ch: 不用担心我！虎克有洞洞机，完全不怕受伤！
t_en: Don't worry about me! I have Diggertron. No one can hurt me!

t_ch: 那就好，那就好…这位{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}呢，有没有受伤？多谢你伸出援手……
t_en: Good, that's good... And you, {F#young lady}{M#young man}, are you hurt? Thank you for your help...

t_ch: 不疼不痒。
t_en: I barely felt a thing.

t_ch: 暂时还死不了。
t_en: I'll live.

t_ch: 啊我想起来了…你就是娜塔莎提过的外来者吧？听说你们刚来就解决了矿山的冲突，没想到今天又被你救了……
t_en: Ah, I remember... You are the outsiders Natasha mentioned before, right? I heard that you helped resolve some conflicts around the mines when you arrived. And again today, when you saved my life...

t_ch: 也没帮上什么大忙。
t_en: It's no big deal.

t_ch: 流浪者和矿队冲突也不是一天两天了…你能帮忙暂时调剂关系，大家已经很感激了。
t_en: The conflicts between the vagrants and the mining team have lasted for quite a long time... Even if you have just temporarily mitigated our rather tense relationship, it is greatly appreciated.

t_ch: 救人是我的爱好。
t_en: Saving lives is my passion.

t_ch: 老爹，我们赶紧去老巫婆的诊所吧，你好像伤得很重！
t_en: Daddy, let's go to the old witch's clinic. You're seriously hurt!

t_ch: 可、可是…我的「矿脉探测器」被偷走了。要是不赶紧去找，恐怕就再也找不到了……
t_en: B—But... the vein detector that rapscallion was after— somebody already stole it from me. I need to keep searching, otherwise I might never see it again...

t_ch: 快去诊所吧，别让虎克担心。
t_en: Head to the clinic. Don't make Hook worry.

t_ch: 仪器重要还是命重要？
t_en: What's more important? The detector or your life?
...*sigh*

t_ch: 不过是件工具而已，丢了就丢了吧。回诊所，回诊所。
t_en: It's just a tool, I guess I should let it go. Let's head to the clinic.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}可别逞强了，你这一身伤得及时处理。
t_en: Quit acting tough, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. Your injuries must be treated right away.

t_ch: 我这一生也算光荣…
t_en: I'm too young to die...

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}别怕，你的伤势没那么严重。
t_en: Don't be afraid, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. Your injury isn't fatal.

t_ch: 喔，你真是高尚的年轻人！谢谢你！
t_en: What a noble young spirit you have! Thank you very much!

t_ch: 我只是想打一架。
t_en: I just wanted a fight.

t_ch: 呵呵，不管是为什么吧…我很感激。
t_en: Haha, whatever your motivation was... I appreciate your help.

t_ch: 少废话，我说去你就去！
t_en: Stop talking nonsense and go to the clinic now!

t_ch: 伤口已经基本处理好了。现在医疗物资紧张，这段时间不要再到处惹事儿了，听到了没？
t_en: I've taken care of your wounds. I'm running short on medical supplies these days though, so behave yourselves and stay away from trouble, you hear me?

t_ch: 别装没听见，我说的就是你——虎克。
t_en: Don't pretend you didn't hear that, Hook, I'm talking to you!

t_ch: …虎克可没惹事，是坏家伙们先动的手！我们只是自卫，{NICKNAME}可以作证！
t_en: ...Hook wasn't the one that caused trouble. It was those bad guys! We were just defending ourselves, ask {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 虎克大人说的都对。
t_en: The boss is always right.

t_ch: 你看吧！
t_en: See!

t_ch: …你这样可是会把小孩子惯坏的。
t_en: ...You are gonna spoil that kid.

t_ch: 可不能惯着孩子…
t_en: We shouldn't indulge kids...

t_ch: 喂，你到底是站哪边的呀！
t_en: Hey, whose side are you on!?

t_ch: 你知道就好，可不能让这孩子由着性子来。
t_en: I'm glad we're on the same page. We can't just let this kid do whatever she wants.

t_ch: 时间差不多了，我再说最后一次，虎克——再在外面惹事的话，我就要没收洞洞机了。听到了没？
t_en: That's enough for now. I'll say this one last time, Hook: If you cause any trouble again, I'll take Diggertron away. Do you understand?

t_ch: 唔…虎克记住了！
t_en: Mmm... Hook understands!

t_ch: 鼹鼠党的老大就得说到做到才行。我还有病历要填，你们先聊着吧。
t_en: The Boss of The Moles should stick to her word. I have patients to take care of, see you later.

t_ch: 多亏了你和娜塔莎医生啊…要不然我们父女俩多半要遭殃了。
t_en: Thank you again... If it weren't for you and Doctor Natasha, Hook and I probably would've been in big trouble.

t_ch: 到底发生了什么事？
t_en: What happened exactly?

t_ch: 前些日子，我用矿脉探测器发现了一处地髓矿脉，然后把它的情报卖给了矿队工头安东尼娜。
t_en: *sigh* Well, a few days ago, I found a Geomarrow vein using a vein detector, and I sold this info to Antonia, a supervisor of the mining team.

t_ch: 那些流浪者听说了这事，就指责我是叛徒…他们觉得，既然我也是从别的矿区沦落至此的流浪者，理所当然就该跟他们站在一边。
t_en: Then those vagrants heard about it and accused me of being a traitor... They believe that since I'm also a vagrant who came from another mine and ended up in here for a living, I should naturally be on their side.

t_ch: 当然了，矿队才不会承认这种说法。嗐，你说这事闹的……
t_en: The mining team could not agree with that, of course. *sigh* I'm too old for this...

t_ch: 你一定靠矿脉的情报赚了不少。
t_en: You must have made a lot of money selling that intel about the vein.

t_ch: …没错，这可是相当值钱的情报。
t_en: Yes indeed, it is quite a valuable piece of information.

t_ch: 这么干是有点儿不地道。
t_en: Can't say what you did was very ethical.

t_ch: 你这么认为？我可不觉得自己欠流浪者什么人情。倒不如说，他们现在能有个落脚的地方，还不是多亏我当时向矿队求情……
t_en: You think so? But I don't believe I owe anything to the vagrants. In fact, when they had no roof over their heads, I begged the mining team to offer them shelter...

t_ch: 哎，我想什么呢？指望那帮混蛋懂得感恩……
t_en: *sigh* I was so naive... How can I expect those rascals to know anything about gratitude...

t_ch: 老爹，我们很缺钱嘛？为什么要这么做？
t_en: Daddy, are we short on money? Why did you do that?

t_ch: 傻孩子…老爹快干不动了，不知道还能陪你多久，总要为以后做做打算了。
t_en: My poor child... Daddy is old now. Who knows how long I can stay by your side? I have to start preparing for your future.

t_ch: 被人说自私也好，背叛也好，这都不重要。重要的是，等我走了以后，虎克你也能靠着这笔钱照顾好自己，生活下去…
t_en: People say I'm selfish, think I'm a traitor, but it doesn't matter to me. All you need to know is, when I'm gone, you will have the money to take care of yourself and survive on your own...

t_ch: 老爹……
t_en: Daddy...

t_ch: 算了，既然矿队的钱已经拿到了，我也没什么可抱怨的。
t_en: Forget about it. Since I've got the money from the mining team, I have nothing to complain about now.

t_ch: 要说有什么可惜的，就是我的矿脉探测器不见了…那可真是个好宝贝啊。
t_en: It's just a shame that I lost my vein detector... It was indeed a treasure.

t_ch: 被人偷走了吗？
t_en: Someone stole it?

t_ch: 我猜也是。那设备是能探测地髓矿脉的古代遗物，非常稀有，我还没见过第二个。
t_en: I guess so. It's an ancient relic that can detect Geomarrow veins. It's very rare — I haven't seen a second one in my life.

t_ch: 可能是因为我常拿它寻找矿脉，结果被不怀好意的家伙盯上了吧？
t_en: I often carried it around looking for a new claim, I suppose some villains must have taken notice.

t_ch: 矿脉探测器是个什么玩意？
t_en: What is a vein detector?

t_ch: 是我在矿坑里捡到的古代遗物，据说是以前的开采团专门拿来探测地髓矿脉的。
t_en: It's an ancient relic that I found in a mine. People say it's a device that mining teams used for detecting Geomarrow veins long ago.

t_ch: 可能是因为我常拿它寻找矿脉，结果被不怀好意的家伙盯上了吧？这么稀有的东西，被偷也是正常的……
t_en: I often carried it around looking for a fresh claim, perhaps some villains up to no good took notice. It's a rare device you know, no wonder somebody stole it...

t_ch: 事到如今，我要探测器也没用了。多一事不如少一事，懒得再追究什么了…偷我东西的家伙，嗐，祝他好运吧……
t_en: ...Anyway, I guess I don't need the detector anymore. The less trouble the better, I shouldn't care who stole my device... *sigh* Whoever it was, I hope they get good use out of it...

t_ch: （虎克好像在用眼神说：「到诊所外面去，虎克有话要说。」）
t_en: {M#Hook seems to be giving me a look like: "I wanna talk to you outside the clinic, now!"}{F#Hook seems to be giving me a look that says: "Hook wants to talk to you outside the clinic, now!"}

t_ch: （这就是会说话的眼睛吗？）
t_en: (So that's why people call eyes the "windows to the soul.")

t_ch: 我不知道，与我无关。
t_en: I don't know. It has nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 喂，你这个叛徒！
t_en: Hmph, you traitor!

t_ch: …你这就把自己撇得一干二净啦。
t_en: ...Well, passing the buck is one way to handle this.

t_ch: 你应该货卖两家，坐山观虎斗。
t_en: Maybe you should've sold the intel to both parties and let them duke it out.

t_ch: …那样的话，我的信誉就彻底完了。
t_en: ...If I had done that, I would've completely destroyed my credibility.

t_ch: …等等，虎克还有事想找你帮忙！
t_en: ...Wait, Hook needs you to help with something!

t_ch: 是费斯曼的事吗？
t_en: Is it about Fersman?

t_ch: 没错！虎克觉得，事情不能就这么结了！
t_en: Yes! We can't just stand by and do nothing!

t_ch: 包在我身上了。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: …我还没说要干什么呢！不过，精神头很足，不愧是鼹鼠党的荣誉队员！
t_en: ...I haven't said what we're gonna do! But that's the spirit. You truly are an honorary member of The Moles!

t_ch: 那个什么探测器，既然那是老爹的宝贝，虎克一定要帮他找回来！你会帮虎克的吧？
t_en: That detector is daddy's treasure, so I gotta get it back! You'll help me, won't you?

t_ch: 提起帮忙我就来劲。
t_en: Helping others is my passion.

t_ch: 你好别扭啊，我就当作你十分愿意帮助虎克啦？
t_en: Don't be a jerk. I'll just take your response as a yes.

t_ch: 太好了！我会在矿区避难所里等着你的，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。
t_en: Alright then! I'll be waiting for you at the shelter in the mine, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}!

t_ch: 知道了。
t_en: Got it.

t_ch: 刚才你叫我什么？
t_en: What did you just call me...?

t_ch: 再见！
t_en: Seeya!
